title: Decision record: PKCS11 put ordering prevents data loss
description:
## Decision
Pkcs11Backend::put() uses find-existing → create-new → destroy-old ordering, not find → destroy → create.

## Rationale
If create-new fails after destroy-old, the secret is gone with no recovery path. The chosen order ensures the new object exists before the old one is removed. The window where both exist simultaneously is acceptable (only one session at a time can hold the RW session for this label). A crash between create and destroy leaves both objects; the label lookup returns whichever was found first, which is implementation-defined — but the secret is not lost.

## Trade-off accepted
Brief window where two objects with the same label exist. Documented in code comments.

## Record this as a confirmed good decision.
